title: Roscoea lutea
intro: Roscoea lutea is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, commonly known as the yellow roscoea. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Roscoea lutea include gingerols, shogaols, and other compounds that have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Roscoea lutea is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support skin health and wound healing.

The plant is a member of the genus Roscoea, which includes approximately 50 species of flowering plants. Roscoea lutea is a perennial herbaceous plant, often found growing in moist, woodland environments.
